title: The Pain That Outlives the Organ
description: In up to 30% of chronic pancreatitis patients, surgically removing the pancreas entirely fails to stop the pain. That's not a surgical failure — it's evidence that the disease has already migrated to the nervous system. This is the biology of pancreatic central sensitization, why medicine keeps treating the wrong target, and why there are still no approved treatments for one of the most undertreated pain conditions in existence.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Pain That Outlives the Organ
